{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/r/Projects/MIX-Project/README.md","content":"# Ray-marching in Thurston geometries

This project started from the ray marching hyperbolic virtual
reality [simulation](https://github.com/mtwoodard/hypVR-Ray_m) (for mobile devices) by Henry Segerman and Michael
Woodard,
which is in turn based on the full [simulation](https://github.com/mtwoodard/hypVR-Ray).

This version is as independent of the underlying geometry as possible.
It comes with an API to

- build and animate scenes in a given geometry
- define objects, lights, materials, etc
- implement new geometries (or new models of existing geometries)

This project started at the *Illustrating Mathematics* semester program at [ICERM](https://icerm.brown.edu).

Visit the website https://3-dimensional.space for demos, math explanations, and documentation.

## Examples

The project contains numerous ready to use examples.
It contains scenes in the various geometries and demons

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/r/Projects/MIX-Project/doc/tutorials/start/index.md","content":"# Getting started


This tutorial explains how to build a simple scene with the *Thurston* module. The basics are very similar
to [Three.js](https://threejs.org/). There are some differences though that we will highlight along the way.

Let's start with a basic HTML file `index.html`

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <style>
        html, body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
        }
    </style>
    <title>My first example</title>
</head>
<body>
</body>
<script type="module" id="main"></script>
</html>
```

At each step of the tutorial, we highlight the additional lines of code needed to perform the described action.
The complete script can be found at the end of this tutorial.

input.content:
---
title: "feat: Get All Dist Demos Running Locally"
type: feat
status: active
date: 2026-02-17
---

# Get All Dist Demos Running Locally

## Overview

The `dist/index.html` lists 10 demo links (8 geometry demos + tutorial + debug loader), but only 2 HTML pages exist (`example_hyp.html`, `tutorial.html`). Both existing pages have broken imports due to webpack externals not re-exporting core symbols through geometry bundles. This plan fixes the existing pages and creates the 6 missing geometry demos + debug page so the full demo index is viewable via a local static file server.

## Problem Statement

1. **6 missing demo pages**: `example_euc.html`, `example_sph.html`, `example_h2e.html`, `example_s2e.html`, `example_nil.html`, `example_sl2.html`, `example_sol.html`, `debug_thurston.html` do not exist
2. **Broken imports in existing pages**: `export * from "thurstonCore"` in geometry bundles only re-exports symbols that webpack statically found during build. `PhongMaterial`, `Color`, `trivialSet`, `BasicCamera`, `Scene`, `FlyControls` etc. are NOT available from geometry bundles - they must be imported from `'thurstonCore'` or `'three'` directly
3. **No serving instructions**: Users need to know how to serve `dist/` locally (ES modules require HTTP, not `file://`)

## Technical Considerations

### The Export Gap (Critical)

When webpack builds a geometry bundle (e.g., `thurstonHyp.js`) with `thurstonCore` as an external, the `export * from "thurstonCore"` statement does NOT create a full wildcard re-export. Instead, webpack statically analyzes which core symbols the geometry bundle's own source references, and only those ~30-50 symbols are re-exported. The full `thurstonCore.js` exports ~100+ symbols.

**Consequence**: Demo pages must use a **two-import pattern**:
- Geometry-specific exports (shapes, lights, group sets) from the geometry bundle
- Shared/core exports (`PhongMaterial`, `Color`, `ThurstonLite`, etc.) from `'thurstonCore'` or `'three'`

### Import Map Strategy

Each demo page needs an import map with:
```json
{
    "imports": {
        "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
        "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
        "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
        "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js",
        "thurstonCore": "./thurston/thurstonCore.js"
    }
}
```
Geometry bundles are loaded via direct relative path (`./thurston/thurstonXxx.js`), not a bare specifier.

### Light Constructor Inconsistency

Constructor argument order differs across geometries:

| Geometry | Light Class | Signature |
|----------|------------|-----------|
| euc, hyp, sph, h2e, s2e | `PointLight` | `(position, color, intensity)` |
| nil | `FakePointLight` | `(position, color, intensity)` |
| sl2 | `LocalFakePointLight` | `(color, position, intensity)` |
| sol | `LocalFakePointLight` | `(color, intensity, position)` |

Each demo page must use the correct argument order for its geometry.

### Shape Availability

| Geometry | Ball Class | Boolean Ops |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| euc, hyp, sph, h2e, s2e | `LocalBallShape` | euc/hyp only |
| nil | `LocalBallShape` (or `LocalFakeBallShape`) | yes |
| sl2 | `LocalFakeBallShape` only | no |
| sol | `LocalFakeBallShape` only | yes |

### Group Sets Per Geometry

| Geometry | Recommended Group Set | Description |
|----------|----------------------|-------------|
| euc | `freeAbelianSet` | Standard cubic lattice tiling |
| hyp | `cubeSet` | Right-angled hyperbolic cube |
| sph | `quaternionSet` | Quaternion group on S3 |
| h2e | `cuspedTorusSet` | Cusped torus in H2xE |
| s2e | `cubeSet` | Cube tiling in S2xE |
| nil | `basicHeisenbergSet` | Basic Heisenberg group |
| sl2 | `fiberLoopSet` | Fiber loop in SL(2,R) |
| sol | `mappingTorusSet` | Mapping torus in Sol |

## Proposed Solution

Use `ThurstonLite` for all 8 geometry demos (batteries-included: camera, renderer, controls, GUI). Each page follows the same HTML template with geometry-specific imports. Fix both existing pages. Create a debug page that verifies all bundles load.

### Demo Page Template Pattern

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - {Geometry Name}</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js",
            "thurstonCore": "./thurston/thurstonCore.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {ThurstonLite, PhongMaterial, Vector, Point} from "thurstonCore";
    import {Color} from "three";
    // Geometry-specific imports from the bundle via direct path:
    import {groupSet, LightClass, ShapeClass} from "./thurston/thurstonXxx.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(groupSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    // ... add lights, shapes, materials
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>
```

## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements

- [ ] `dist/index.html` loads in browser and all 10 links resolve to existing pages
- [ ] `example_hyp.html` renders a hyperbolic scene with visible geometry
- [ ] `example_euc.html` renders a Euclidean scene
- [ ] `example_sph.html` renders a spherical scene
- [ ] `example_h2e.html` renders an H2xE scene
- [ ] `example_s2e.html` renders an S2xE scene
- [ ] `example_nil.html` renders a Nil scene
- [ ] `example_sl2.html` renders an SL(2,R) scene
- [ ] `example_sol.html` renders a Sol scene
- [ ] `tutorial.html` renders a tutorial Euclidean scene
- [ ] `debug_thurston.html` reports load status of all 9 bundles
- [ ] All pages work when served via `python3 -m http.server` from `dist/`
- [ ] Keyboard navigation (WASD) works in all demo pages

### Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] Consistent HTML structure across all 8 geometry demos
- [ ] Each page self-contained (no external network requests)
- [ ] Pages load in < 5s on modern hardware
- [ ] No console errors in Chrome/Firefox/Safari

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Fix Existing Pages

**Files to modify:**

#### `dist/example_hyp.html`

Current broken pattern:
```js
const { ThurstonLite, cubeSet, PointLight, PhongMaterial,
        LocalBallShape, complement, Solid, phongWrap,
        Vector, Point, Color } = thurstonModule;
```
Fix: Import `ThurstonLite`, `PhongMaterial`, `Vector`, `Point` from `'thurstonCore'`. Import `Color` from `'three'`. Import `cubeSet`, `PointLight`, `LocalBallShape`, `complement`, `Solid` from `./thurston/thurstonHyp.js`. Switch from dynamic import to static import.

#### `dist/tutorial.html`

Current broken pattern:
```js
import { trivialSet, BasicCamera, BasicRenderer, Scene,
         PointLight, Point, PhongMaterial, Ball, FlyControls
       } from "thurstonEuc";
```
Fix: Import `trivialSet`, `BasicCamera`, `BasicRenderer`, `Scene`, `PhongMaterial`, `FlyControls` from `'thurstonCore'`. Import `PointLight`, `Point`, `Ball` from `./thurston/thurstonEuc.js`.

### Phase 2: Create 6 Missing Geometry Demos

Each follows the template pattern. Geometry-specific details:

#### `dist/example_euc.html`
- Bundle: `./thurston/thurstonEuc.js`
- Group set: `freeAbelianSet`
- Shape: `LocalBallShape(new Point(0, 0, -2), 0.5)`
- Light: `PointLight(new Vector(-1, 1, -2), new Color(1, 1, 1))`

#### `dist/example_sph.html`
- Bundle: `./thurston/thurstonSph.js`
- Group set: `quaternionSet`
- Shape: `LocalBallShape(new Point(), 0.3)` (origin in S3)
- Light: `PointLight(new Vector(1, 0, 0), new Color(1, 1, 1))`

#### `dist/example_h2e.html`
- Bundle: `./thurston/thurstonH2E.js`
- Group set: `cuspedTorusSet`
- Shape: `LocalBallShape(new Point(0, 0, 0), 0.3)`
- Light: `PointLight(new Vector(0, 1, -1), new Color(1, 1, 1))`

#### `dist/example_s2e.html`
- Bundle: `./thurston/thurstonS2E.js`
- Group set: `cubeSet`
- Shape: `LocalBallShape(new Point(0, 0, 0), 0.3)`
- Light: `PointLight(new Vector(1, 0, -1), new Color(1, 1, 1))`

#### `dist/example_nil.html`
- Bundle: `./thurston/thurstonNil.js`
- Group set: `basicHeisenbergSet`
- Shape: `LocalBallShape(new Point(0, 0, 0), 0.3)` (Nil has true LocalBallShape)
- Light: `FakePointLight(new Vector(1, 0, -1), new Color(1, 1, 1))` (position first)

#### `dist/example_sl2.html`
- Bundle: `./thurston/thurstonSL2.js`
- Group set: `fiberLoopSet`
- Shape: `LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(0, 0, 0), 0.3)`
- Light: `LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Vector(1, 0, -1))` (color first, then position)

#### `dist/example_sol.html`
- Bundle: `./thurston/thurstonSol.js`
- Group set: `mappingTorusSet`
- Shape: `LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(0, 0, 0), 0.3)`
- Light: `LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), 1, new Vector(0, 0, -1))` (color, intensity, position)

### Phase 3: Create Debug Page

#### `dist/debug_thurston.html`

A diagnostic page that:
1. Attempts to dynamically import all 9 bundles (`thurstonCore` + 8 geometries)
2. Reports success/failure for each with a green/red indicator
3. Tests that key exports exist (`ThurstonLite`, `bindGeometry` from core; geometry-specific shapes/lights from each bundle)
4. Shows import map resolution status
5. No 3D rendering - purely a module loading health check

### Phase 4: Verify & Document

1. Serve `dist/` with `python3 -m http.server 8000`
2. Open `http://localhost:8000/` and click through all 10 links
3. Verify each geometry renders without console errors
4. Verify keyboard navigation works (WASD)
5. Add a brief serving instruction to `dist/index.html` (`<pre>` block at bottom)

## Dependencies & Risks

### Dependencies
- All 9 geometry bundles must be pre-built in `dist/thurston/` (they currently are)
- All vendor files must exist in `dist/vendor/` (they currently do)
- No npm install or webpack build needed - all pages use pre-built bundles

### Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| Geometry bundle doesn't export expected symbol | Medium | High | Verify each import against actual bundle before writing demo |
| Light constructor arg order mismatch causes silent wrong lighting | High (known issue) | Medium | Document exact signatures per geometry in each demo's comments |
| Some geometries crash on certain shapes/group sets | Low | Medium | Test each page individually; fall back to simpler scene if needed |
| Browser compatibility (import maps) | Low | Medium | Include `es-module-shims.js` polyfill in all pages |

## References & Research

### Internal References
- Existing working demo: `dist/example_hyp.html` (broken imports, but correct HTML structure)
- Tutorial with manual setup: `dist/tutorial.html` (broken imports)
- Dev scene (working, comprehensive): `dev/dev.js` - shows advanced hyp scene
- Webpack externals config: `webpack/webpack.prod.gen.js`
- Core exports chain: `src/thurstonCore.js` -> `src/core.js` -> `src/commons/*/all.js`

### Key File Paths
- Geometry bundles: `dist/thurston/thurston{Core,Euc,Hyp,Sph,H2E,S2E,Nil,SL2,Sol}.js`
- Vendor files: `dist/vendor/{three,stats,dat.gui,webxr-polyfill,es-module-shims}.module.js`
- Source entry points: `src/thurston{Core,Euc,Hyp,Sph,H2E,S2E,Nil,SL2,Sol}.js`
- Geometry shapes: `src/geometries/{geom}/shapes/all.js`
- Geometry lights: `src/geometries/{geom}/lights/all.js`
- Group sets: `src/geometries/{geom}/groups/*/set.js`

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Hyperbolic (H3)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js",
            "thurstonCore": "./thurston/thurstonCore.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    // Core symbols from thurstonCore (not re-exported by geometry bundles)
    import {ThurstonLite, PhongMaterial, Vector, Point} from "thurstonCore";
    import {Color} from "three";
    // Geometry-specific: group set, lights, shapes
    import {cubeSet, PointLight, LocalBallShape} from "./thurston/thurstonHyp.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(cubeSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, 0, 0), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(0, 1, -1), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light2 = new PointLight(new Vector(-1, -1, 1), new Color(1, 1, 1));

    const mat = new PhongMaterial({color: new Color(0.2, 0.5, 0.9), shininess: 10});
    const ball = new LocalBallShape(new Point(0, 0, 0), 0.5, mat);

    thurston.add(ball, light0, light1, light2);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Thurston Tutorial - Euclidean</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js",
            "thurstonCore": "./thurston/thurstonCore.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    // Core symbols (renderer, camera, scene, controls, materials, trivialSet)
    import {
        trivialSet, BasicCamera, BasicRenderer, Scene,
        PhongMaterial, FlyControls
    } from "thurstonCore";
    import {Color, Clock} from "three";
    // Geometry-specific: lights, shapes, Point
    import {PointLight, Point, Ball} from "./thurston/thurstonEuc.js";

    const camera = new BasicCamera({set: trivialSet});
    const scene = new Scene();
    const renderer = new BasicRenderer(trivialSet, camera, scene);

    const controls = new FlyControls(camera);
    const clock = new Clock();

    renderer.setPixelRatio(window.devicePixelRatio);
    renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);
    document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement);

    const light = new PointLight(
        new Point(-1, 1, -2),
        new Color(0, 1, 1),
        0.5
    );
    const mat = new PhongMaterial({shininess: 10});
    const ball = new Ball(
        new Point(-1, -0.5, -2),
        0.3,
        mat
    );

    scene.add(light, ball);
    renderer.build();

    function animate() {
        const delta = clock.getDelta();
        controls.update(delta);
        renderer.render();
    }

    renderer.setAnimationLoop(animate);
    renderer.checkShader();
</script>
</html>

<script type="module">
    import {PhongMaterial} from "thurstonCore";
    import {Color} from "three";
    // Sol has only FakeBallShape and LocalFakePointLight
    // Sol LocalFakePointLight signature: (color, intensity, position)
    import {
        ThurstonLite, mappingTorusSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, LocalFakeBallShape,
        Vector, Point
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSol.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(mappingTorusSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    // Sol LocalFakePointLight: (color, intensity, position)
    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), 1, new Vector(0, 0, -1));
    const light1 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 0.9, 0.8), 1, new Vector(1, 1, 0));

    const mat = new PhongMaterial({color: new Color(0.8, 0.6, 0.2), shininess: 10});
    const ball = new LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(0, 0, 0), 0.3, mat);

    thurston.add(ball, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

Be very thorough - read all these files.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Thurston Tutorial - Euclidean</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    // All imports from geometry bundle (core is now included)
    import {
        trivialSet, BasicCamera, BasicRenderer, Scene,
        PhongMaterial, FlyControls,
        PointLight, Point, Ball
    } from "./thurston/thurstonEuc.js";
    import {Color, Clock} from "three";

    const camera = new BasicCamera({set: trivialSet});
    const scene = new Scene();
    const renderer = new BasicRenderer(trivialSet, camera, scene);

    const controls = new FlyControls(camera);
    const clock = new Clock();

    renderer.setPixelRatio(window.devicePixelRatio);
    renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight);
    document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement);

    const light = new PointLight(
        new Point(-1, 1, -2),
        new Color(0, 1, 1),
        0.5
    );
    const mat = new PhongMaterial({shininess: 10});
    const ball = new Ball(
        new Point(-1, -0.5, -2),
        0.3,
        mat
    );

    scene.add(light, ball);
    renderer.build();

    function animate() {
        const delta = clock.getDelta();
        controls.update(delta);
        renderer.render();
    }

    renderer.setAnimationLoop(animate);
    renderer.checkShader();
</script>
</html>

input.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Hyperbolic (H3)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    // Replicates the working dev.js lattice scene
    import {
        ThurstonLite, cubeSet,
        PointLight, LocalBallShape, LocalHoroballShape,
        complement, union, Solid,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Vector, Point, Color, CREEPING_FULL
    } from "./thurston/thurstonHyp.js";

    cubeSet.creepingType = CREEPING_FULL;
    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(cubeSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    thurston.renderer.maxBounces = 1;

    // Lights
    const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, 0, 0), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(0, 1, -1), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light2 = new PointLight(new Vector(-1, -1, 1), new Color(1, 1, 1));

    // Material
    const latticeColor = new Color(0.2, 0.5, 0.95);
    const latticeBaseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(
        latticeColor, new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2)
    );
    const latticeMat = new PhongWrapMaterial(latticeBaseMat, {
        color: latticeColor,
        shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.3, 0.3, 0.3),
    });

    // Lattice complement: ball at center + horoballs at cube vertices
    const centerBall = new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 1.02);
    const modelHalfCube = 1 / Math.sqrt(3);
    const horoballs = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        const i0 = i % 2;
        const i1 = 0.5 * (i - i0) % 2;
        const i2 = 0.25 * (i - 2 * i1 - i0) % 2;
        horoballs[i] = new LocalHoroballShape(
            new Vector(
                (2 * i0 - 1) * modelHalfCube,
                (2 * i1 - 1) * modelHalfCube,
                (2 * i2 - 1) * modelHalfCube
            ),
            0.98
        );
    }
    const lattice = new Solid(complement(union(centerBall, ...horoballs)), latticeMat);

    thurston.add(lattice, light0, light1, light2);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

input.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Euclidean (E3)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, freeAbelianSet,
        PointLight, Ball, Solid,
        LocalBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonEuc.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(freeAbelianSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(-1, 1, -2), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, -1, -1), new Color(0.8, 0.8, 1));

    // Complement of a ball at origin creates visible lattice walls
    const wallColor = new Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.2);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 0.9)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Spherical (S3)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, quaternionSet,
        PointLight, Ball,
        PhongMaterial, Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSph.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(quaternionSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, 0, 0), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(0, 1, -1), new Color(0.8, 1, 0.8));

    const mat = new PhongMaterial({color: new Color(0.3, 0.8, 0.4), shininess: 10});
    // Balls at different positions so at least some are visible
    const ball0 = new Ball(new Point(0, 0, -1), 0.3, mat);
    const ball1 = new Ball(new Point(1, 0, 0), 0.3, mat);
    const ball2 = new Ball(new Point(0, 1, 0), 0.3, mat);

    thurston.add(ball0, ball1, ball2, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Nil</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>

            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, basicHeisenbergSet,
        FakePointLight, Solid,
        LocalBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonNil.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(basicHeisenbergSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    // Nil FakePointLight: (position, color, intensity)
    const light0 = new FakePointLight(new Vector(1, 0, -1), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new FakePointLight(new Vector(-1, 1, 0), new Color(0.8, 1, 1));

    // Complement of a ball creates visible walls
    const wallColor = new Color(0.2, 0.7, 0.7);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - SL(2,R)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, fiberLoopSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, FakeBall,
        PhongMaterial, Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSL2.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(fiberLoopSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    // SL2 LocalFakePointLight: (color, position, intensity)
    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Vector(1, 0, -1));
    const light1 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(0.8, 0.8, 1), new Vector(-1, 1, 0));

    const mat = new PhongMaterial({color: new Color(0.7, 0.3, 0.5), shininess: 10});
    const ball0 = new FakeBall(new Point(0, 0, -0.5), 0.3, mat);
    const ball1 = new FakeBall(new Point(0.5, 0.5, 0), 0.3, mat);
    const ball2 = new FakeBall(new Point(-0.5, 0, 0.3), 0.3, mat);

    thurston.add(ball0, ball1, ball2, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Sol</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, mappingTorusSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, Solid,
        LocalFakeBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSol.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(mappingTorusSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    // Sol LocalFakePointLight: (color, intensity, position)
    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), 1, new Vector(0, 0, -1));
    const light1 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 0.9, 0.8), 1, new Vector(1, 1, 0));

    // Complement of a ball creates visible walls
    const wallColor = new Color(0.8, 0.6, 0.2);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

input.old_string:
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, freeAbelianSet,
        PointLight, Ball, Solid,
        LocalBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonEuc.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(freeAbelianSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(-1, 1, -2), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, -1, -1), new Color(0.8, 0.8, 1));

    // Complement of a ball at origin creates visible lattice walls
    const wallColor = new Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.2);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 0.9)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
input.new_string:
<script type="module">
    try {
        document.body.style.background = '#000';
        const msg = (t) => { const d = document.createElement('pre'); d.style.cssText='color:lime;padding:10px;font-size:14px'; d.textContent=t; document.body.appendChild(d); };
        msg('Loading thurstonEuc.js...');

        const mod = await import("./thurston/thurstonEuc.js");
        msg('Module loaded. Checking exports...');
        const needed = ['ThurstonLite','freeAbelianSet','PointLight','Ball','Solid','LocalBallShape','complement','PhongWrapMaterial','VaryingColorMaterial','Vector','Point','Color'];
        const missing = needed.filter(n => !mod[n]);
        if (missing.length) { msg('MISSING: ' + missing.join(', ')); }
        else { msg('All exports OK'); }

        const {ThurstonLite, freeAbelianSet, PointLight, Solid, LocalBallShape, complement, PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial, Vector, Point, Color} = mod;

        msg('Creating ThurstonLite...');
        const thurston = new ThurstonLite(freeAbelianSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
        msg('ThurstonLite created OK');

        const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(-1, 1, -2), new Color(1, 1, 1));
        const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, -1, -1), new Color(0.8, 0.8, 1));
        msg('Lights created OK');

        const wallColor = new Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.2);
        const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
        const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {color: wallColor, shininess: 5, reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2)});
        const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 0.9)), mat);
        msg('Scene objects created OK');

        thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
        msg('Scene populated. Calling run()...');
        thurston.run();
    } catch(e) {
        document.body.style.background = '#200';
        const d = document.createElement('pre');
        d.style.cssText='color:red;padding:20px;font-size:16px;white-space:pre-wrap';
        d.textContent = 'ERROR: ' + e.message + '\n\n' + e.stack;
        document.body.appendChild(d);
    }
</script>

import ssl

handler = SimpleHTTPRequestHandler
handler.extensions_map = {
    '.txt': 'text/plain',
    '.html': 'text/html',
    '.png': 'image/png',
	'.jpg': 'image/jpg',
	'.svg':	'image/svg+xml',
	'.css':	'text/css',
	'.js':	'text/javascript',
	'.mjs':	'text/javascript',
	'': 'application/octet-stream', # Default
}

httpd = HTTPServer(('localhost', 4443), handler)


httpd.socket = ssl.wrap_socket (httpd.socket,
        keyfile="./ssl/key.pem",
        certfile='./ssl/cert.pem', server_side=True)

httpd.serve_forever()

{"stdout":"# 3-dimensional space

This project started from the ray marching hyperbolic virtual
reality [simulation](https://github.com/mtwoodard/hypVR-Ray_m) (for mobile devices) by Henry Segerman and Michael
Woodard,
which is in turn based on the full [simulation](https://github.com/mtwoodard/hypVR-Ray).
This software implement the ray-marching algorithm to render inside view of the eight Thurston geometries.

This version is as independent of the underlying geometry as possible.
It comes with an API to

- build and animate scenes in a given geometry
- define objects, lights, materials, etc
- implement new geometries (or new models of existing geometries)

This project started at the *Illustrating Mathematics* semester program at [ICERM](https://icerm.brown.edu).

Visit the website https://3-dimensional.space for demos, math explanations, and documentation.

## Examples

The project contains numerous ready to use examples.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Euclidean (E3)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, freeAbelianSet,
        PointLight, Solid,
        LocalBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonEuc.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(freeAbelianSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    // Euc PointLight: (position: Point, color, intensity)
    const light0 = new PointLight(new Point(-1, 1, -2, 1), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Point(1, -1, -1, 1), new Color(0.8, 0.8, 1));

    // Complement of a ball creates visible lattice walls
    const wallColor = new Color(0.9, 0.3, 0.2);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 0.9)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Spherical (S3)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, quaternionSet,
        PointLight, Ball,
        PhongMaterial, Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSph.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(quaternionSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    // Sph PointLight: (position: Point|Vector, color, intensity)
    const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, 0, 0), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(0, 1, -1), new Color(0.8, 1, 0.8));

    const mat = new PhongMaterial({color: new Color(0.3, 0.8, 0.4), shininess: 10});
    // Ball accepts Vector via makeTranslationFromDir
    const ball0 = new Ball(new Vector(0, 0, -1), 0.3, mat);
    const ball1 = new Ball(new Vector(1, 0, 0), 0.3, mat);
    const ball2 = new Ball(new Vector(0, 1, 0), 0.3, mat);

    thurston.add(ball0, ball1, ball2, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

input.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Hyperbolic (H3)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, cubeSet,
        PointLight, Solid,
        LocalBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Vector, Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonHyp.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(cubeSet, {keyboard: 'us'});

    // Performance: halve resolution on Retina, reduce ray-march steps
    thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
    thurston.camera.maxSteps = 50;
    thurston.camera.maxDist = 20;

    // Lights
    const light0 = new PointLight(new Vector(1, 0, 0), new Color(1, 1, 1));
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Vector(0, 1, -1), new Color(1, 1, 1));

    // Simple complement room (much lighter than full lattice)
    const wallColor = new Color(0.2, 0.5, 0.95);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.3, 0.3, 0.3),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 1.02)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

input.old_string:
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, mappingTorusSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, Solid,
        LocalFakeBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSol.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(mappingTorusSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
    thurston.camera.maxSteps = 50;
    thurston.camera.maxDist = 20;

    // Sol LocalFakePointLight: (color, intensity, position: Point)
    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), 1, new Point(0, 0, -1, 1));
    const light1 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 0.9, 0.8), 1, new Point(1, 1, 0, 1));

    const wallColor = new Color(0.8, 0.6, 0.2);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
input.new_string:
<script type="module">
    const overlay = document.createElement('div');
    overlay.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;top:0;left:0;right:0;z-index:99999;background:rgba(0,0,0,0.85);padding:10px;max-height:80vh;overflow:auto;font-family:monospace;font-size:13px';
    document.body.appendChild(overlay);
    const msg = (t, color='lime') => { console.log(t); const d = document.createElement('div'); d.style.cssText='color:'+color+';padding:2px 0'; d.textContent=t; overlay.appendChild(d); };
    window.onerror = (m,s,l,c,e) => msg('GLOBAL: ' + m + '\n' + (e&&e.stack||''), 'red');

    try {
        msg('1. Loading thurstonSol.js...');
        const mod = await import("./thurston/thurstonSol.js");
        msg('2. Module loaded');

        const {ThurstonLite, mappingTorusSet, LocalFakePointLight, Solid, LocalFakeBallShape, complement, PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial, Point, Color} = mod;
        msg('3. Imports OK. mappingTorusSet: ' + (mappingTorusSet ? 'yes' : 'MISSING'));
        msg('   LocalFakePointLight: ' + (LocalFakePointLight ? 'yes' : 'MISSING'));
        msg('   LocalFakeBallShape: ' + (LocalFakeBallShape ? 'yes' : 'MISSING'));
        msg('   complement: ' + (complement ? 'yes' : 'MISSING'));

        msg('4. Creating ThurstonLite...');
        const thurston = new ThurstonLite(mappingTorusSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
        thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
        thurston.camera.maxSteps = 50;
        thurston.camera.maxDist = 20;
        msg('5. ThurstonLite OK');

        msg('6. Creating lights...');
        const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), 1, new Point(0, 0, -1, 1));
        const light1 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 0.9, 0.8), 1, new Point(1, 1, 0, 1));
        msg('7. Lights OK');

        msg('8. Creating room...');
        const wallColor = new Color(0.8, 0.6, 0.2);
        const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
        const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {color: wallColor, shininess: 5, reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2)});
        const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);
        msg('9. Room OK');

        thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
        msg('10. Calling run()...');
        thurston.run();
        msg('11. run() returned');
        setTimeout(() => { overlay.style.display = 'none'; }, 8000);
    } catch(e) {
        msg('CATCH: ' + e.message + '\n' + e.stack, 'red');
    }
</script>

Review the following GPU ASCII rendering implementation for correctness, Three.js r158 compatibility, and potential runtime issues. Focus on:

1. GLSL shader correctness (GLSL ES 3.0 via Three.js `glslVersion: GLSL3`)
2. Three.js uniform array handling for vec4[80] and vec2[80]

5. Any WebGL state management issues

Context: This is a Two-pass GPU ASCII renderer for Three.js r158 (not r174). It uses EffectComposer with a custom Pass subclass. Pass 1 runs at low-res grid resolution, sampling the scene texture and matching to nearest character vector. Pass 2 runs at full resolution, looking up glyphs from a runtime-generated font atlas.

The project uses `three/examples/jsm/` import paths (not `three/addons/`) and webpack with `webpack-glsl-loader` + `strip-whitespace-loader` + `webpack-comment-remover-loader` for GLSL files.

Report ONLY high-confidence issues that would cause runtime failures or incorrect rendering. Skip style suggestions.

input.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - Sol</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, mappingTorusSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, Solid,
        LocalCubeShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSol.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(mappingTorusSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
    thurston.camera.maxSteps = 50;
    thurston.camera.maxDist = 20;

    // Sol LocalFakePointLight: (color, intensity, position: Point)
    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), 1, new Point(0.3, 0.3, -0.3, 1));
    const light1 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(0.8, 0.8, 1), 0.8, new Point(-0.3, -0.3, 0.3, 1));

    const wallColor = new Color(0.8, 0.5, 0.2);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalCubeShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - H2 x E</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, cuspedTorusSet,
        PointLight, Solid,
        LocalBallShape, complement,
        PhongWrapMaterial, VaryingColorMaterial,
        Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonH2E.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(cuspedTorusSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
    thurston.camera.maxSteps = 50;
    thurston.camera.maxDist = 20;

    // H2E PointLight: (color, position: Point, intensity)
    // H2E origin is (0,0,1,0). Offset along E-direction (w)
    const light0 = new PointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Point(0, 0, 1, 0.5), 1);
    const light1 = new PointLight(new Color(0.8, 0.9, 1), new Point(0, 0, 1, -0.5), 0.8);

    const wallColor = new Color(0.4, 0.6, 0.9);
    const baseMat = new VaryingColorMaterial(wallColor, new Color(0.15, 0.15, 0.15));
    const mat = new PhongWrapMaterial(baseMat, {
        color: wallColor, shininess: 5,
        reflectivity: new Color(0.2, 0.2, 0.2),
    });
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0, light1);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - SL(2,R)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>

<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, fiberLoopSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, Solid, FakeBall,
        NormalMaterial,
        Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSL2.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(fiberLoopSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
    thurston.camera.maxSteps = 20;
    thurston.camera.maxDist = 8;

    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Point(0.3, 0, 0, 1, 0.3), 1);

    // Place a visible ball slightly offset from origin
    const mat = new NormalMaterial();
    const ball1 = new FakeBall(new Point(0.3, 0.3, 0, 1, 0), 0.3, mat);
    const ball2 = new FakeBall(new Point(-0.3, 0, 0.3, 1, 0), 0.3, mat);
    const ball3 = new FakeBall(new Point(0, -0.3, 0, 1, 0.3), 0.3, mat);

    thurston.add(ball1, ball2, ball3, light0);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

        msg('FakeBall exists: ' + !!FakeBall);
        msg('complement exists: ' + !!complement);
        msg('NormalMaterial exists: ' + !!NormalMaterial);

        const thurston = new ThurstonLite(fiberLoopSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
        thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
        thurston.camera.maxSteps = 30;
        thurston.camera.maxDist = 10;
        msg('ThurstonLite OK');

        const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Point(), 1);
        msg('Light OK');

        // Try just a ball at origin with SingleColorMaterial
        const mat = new SingleColorMaterial(new Color(1, 0.5, 0));
        const shape = new FakeBallShape(new Point(), 0.5);
        msg('Shape created: ' + shape.uniformType);
        const ball = new Solid(shape, mat);
        msg('Solid OK');

        thurston.add(ball, light0);
        msg('Calling run()...');
        thurston.run();
        msg('run() returned');
        setTimeout(() => { overlay.style.display = 'none'; }, 10000);
    } catch(e) {
        msg('ERROR: ' + e.message + '\n' + e.stack, 'red');
    }
</script>
</body>
</html>

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - SL(2,R)</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonLite, fiberLoopSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, Solid,
        LocalFakeBallShape, complement,
        NormalMaterial,
        Point, Color
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSL2.js";

    const thurston = new ThurstonLite(fiberLoopSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    thurston.setPixelRatio(1);
    // SL2 fake SDF underestimates distance - needs many more steps
    thurston.camera.maxSteps = 150;
    thurston.camera.maxDist = 20;

    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Point(), 1);

    const mat = new NormalMaterial();
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    thurston.add(room, light0);
    thurston.run();
</script>
</body>
</html>

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/r/Projects/MIX-Project/README.md","content":"# Ray-marching in Thurston geometries

This project started from the ray marching hyperbolic virtual
reality [simulation](https://github.com/mtwoodard/hypVR-Ray_m) (for mobile devices) by Henry Segerman and Michael
Woodard,
which is in turn based on the full [simulation](https://github.com/mtwoodard/hypVR-Ray).

This version is as independent of the underlying geometry as possible.
It comes with an API to

- build and animate scenes in a given geometry
- define objects, lights, materials, etc
- implement new geometries (or new models of existing geometries)

This project started at the *Illustrating Mathematics* semester program at [ICERM](https://icerm.brown.edu).

Visit the website https://3-dimensional.space for demos, math explanations, and documentation.

## Examples

The project contains numerous ready to use examples.
It contains scenes in the various geometries and demons

{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/r/Projects/MIX-Project/doc/tutorials/start/index.md","content":"# Getting started


This tutorial explains how to build a simple scene with the *Thurston* module. The basics are very similar
to [Three.js](https://threejs.org/). There are some differences though that we will highlight along the way.

Let's start with a basic HTML file `index.html`

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <style>
        html, body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
        }
    </style>
    <title>My first example</title>
</head>
<body>
</body>
<script type="module" id="main"></script>
</html>
```

At each step of the tutorial, we highlight the additional lines of code needed to perform the described action.
The complete script can be found at the end of this tutorial.

import {Position} from "../geometry/Position.js";
import {GroupElement} from "../geometry/GroupElement.js";
import {Vector} from "../geometry/Vector.js";



/**
 *
 * @classdesc
 * Relative position.
 * A general position is represented as a pair $(h,p)$ where
 *
 * - $h$ (`cellBoost`) is a GroupElement representing an element of a discrete subgroups
 * - $p$ (`local`) is a Position
 *
 * The frame represented by the relative position is the image by $h$ of the frame represented by the position $p$
 *
 * We split the isometry part $hg$ in two pieces.
 * The idea is that $g$ should give a position in the fundamental domain of the (implicit) underlying lattice.
 * This will keep the coordinates of $g$ in a bounded range.
 *
 * For simplicity, we also keep track of the inverse of the cellBoost.
 */

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - SL(2,R) Recording</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script src="vendor/CCapture.all.min.js"></script>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonRecord, fiberLoopSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, Solid,
        LocalFakeBallShape, complement,
        NormalMaterial, Vector,
        Point, Color,
        AsciiPass, defaultAlphabet
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSL2.js";

    const recorder = new ThurstonRecord(fiberLoopSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    recorder.setPixelRatio(1);
    recorder.camera.maxSteps = 50;
    recorder.camera.maxDist = 15;

    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Point(), 1);

    const mat = new NormalMaterial();
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    recorder.add(room, light0);

    // Auto camera path: gentle drift with rotation
    let elapsed = 0;
    recorder.callback = function() {
        elapsed += 0.016; // ~60fps timestep for smooth path
        const speed = 0.003;
        // Spiral drift: forward + slight rotation
        const dx = Math.sin(elapsed * 0.3) * speed;
        const dy = Math.cos(elapsed * 0.5) * speed;
        const dz = -speed * 0.5; // slow forward drift
        const direction = new Vector(dx, dy, dz);
        recorder.camera.position.flow(direction);
    };

    recorder.run();

    // ASCII effect at half cell size (24px) with color mode
    const asciiPass = new AsciiPass({
        alphabetData: defaultAlphabet,
        cellSize: 24,
        colorMode: true
    });
    recorder.renderer.composer.addPass(asciiPass);

    // Status overlay
    const info = document.createElement('div');
    info.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;bottom:10px;left:10px;color:lime;font-family:monospace;font-size:14px;z-index:99999;background:rgba(0,0,0,0.7);padding:8px;border-radius:4px';
    info.textContent = 'Press R to start/stop recording | Camera auto-flying';
    document.body.appendChild(info);
</script>
</body>
</html>

prompt: try the sl2 recording page. once it starts it becomes slow so just plan going straight for a few seconds, then roating 350 degrees, going straight again, then do the same but on a differnt angle.

input.content:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Thurston - SL(2,R) Recording</title>
    <style>html, body { margin: 0; padding: 0; overflow: hidden; }</style>
    <script src="vendor/CCapture.all.min.js"></script>
    <script async src="vendor/es-module-shims.js"></script>
    <script type="importmap">
    {
        "imports": {
            "three": "./vendor/three.module.js",
            "stats": "./vendor/stats.module.js",
            "dat.gui": "./vendor/dat.gui.module.js",
            "webxr-polyfill": "./vendor/webxr-polyfill.module.js"
        }
    }
    </script>
</head>
<body>
<script type="module">
    import {
        ThurstonRecord, fiberLoopSet,
        LocalFakePointLight, Solid,
        LocalFakeBallShape, complement,
        NormalMaterial, Vector,
        Point, Color,
        AsciiPass, defaultAlphabet
    } from "./thurston/thurstonSL2.js";

    import {Quaternion, Vector3} from "three";

    const recorder = new ThurstonRecord(fiberLoopSet, {keyboard: 'us'});
    recorder.setPixelRatio(1);
    recorder.camera.maxSteps = 50;
    recorder.camera.maxDist = 15;

    const light0 = new LocalFakePointLight(new Color(1, 1, 1), new Point(), 1);

    const mat = new NormalMaterial();
    const room = new Solid(complement(new LocalFakeBallShape(new Point(), 0.8)), mat);

    recorder.add(room, light0);

    // Scripted camera path
    // Phase 1 (0-3s):   Go straight forward
    // Phase 2 (3-7s):   Rotate 350 degrees (yaw)
    // Phase 3 (7-10s):  Go straight forward
    // Phase 4 (10-14s): Rotate 350 degrees on different axis (pitch)
    // Phase 5 (14-17s): Go straight forward
    // Total: ~17 seconds at 24fps = 408 frames

    const FPS = 24;
    const DT = 1 / FPS;
    let frame = 0;

    const forwardSpeed = 0.008;
    // 350 degrees = 350/180 * PI radians
    const totalRotation = (350 / 180) * Math.PI;

    recorder.callback = function() {
        frame++;
        const t = frame * DT;

        if (t < 3) {
            // Phase 1: go straight
            recorder.camera.position.flow(new Vector(0, 0, -forwardSpeed));
        } else if (t < 7) {
            // Phase 2: rotate 350 degrees around Y (yaw) over 4 seconds
            const rotPerFrame = totalRotation / (4 * FPS);
            const q = new Quaternion().setFromAxisAngle(new Vector3(0, 1, 0), rotPerFrame);
            recorder.camera.applyQuaternion(q);
            // tiny forward drift while rotating
            recorder.camera.position.flow(new Vector(0, 0, -forwardSpeed * 0.2));
        } else if (t < 10) {
            // Phase 3: go straight
            recorder.camera.position.flow(new Vector(0, 0, -forwardSpeed));
        } else if (t < 14) {
            // Phase 4: rotate 350 degrees around X (pitch) over 4 seconds
            const rotPerFrame = totalRotation / (4 * FPS);
            const q = new Quaternion().setFromAxisAngle(new Vector3(1, 0, 0), rotPerFrame);
            recorder.camera.applyQuaternion(q);
            // tiny forward drift while rotating
            recorder.camera.position.flow(new Vector(0, 0, -forwardSpeed * 0.2));
        } else if (t < 17) {
            // Phase 5: go straight
            recorder.camera.position.flow(new Vector(0, 0, -forwardSpeed));
        } else {
            // Done - stop recording if active
            if (recorder.isRecordOn) {
                recorder.recordStop();
                info.textContent = 'Recording complete! Saving...';
                info.style.color = 'yellow';
            }
        }

        // Update status
        if (t <= 17) {
            const phase = t < 3 ? '1/5 Forward' :
                          t < 7 ? '2/5 Rotating (yaw)' :
                          t < 10 ? '3/5 Forward' :
                          t < 14 ? '4/5 Rotating (pitch)' :
                          '5/5 Forward';
            info.textContent = `${phase} | ${t.toFixed(1)}s / 17s | Frame ${frame} | Press R to record`;
        }
    };

    recorder.run();

    // ASCII effect - chunky colored characters
    const asciiPass = new AsciiPass({
        alphabetData: defaultAlphabet,
        cellSize: 48,
        colorMode: true
    });
    recorder.renderer.composer.addPass(asciiPass);

    // Status overlay
    const info = document.createElement('div');
    info.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;bottom:10px;left:10px;color:lime;font-family:monospace;font-size:14px;z-index:99999;background:rgba(0,0,0,0.7);padding:8px;border-radius:4px';
    info.textContent = 'Loading... Press R to start recording';
    document.body.appendChild(info);
</script>
</body>
</html>
